my_thoughts: The refinement works: super-effort = conscious + purposive + non-required (complete definition), ordinary effort = pretence (devalued). The student requested distinction (seeking criterion for difference), which shows they understand: super-effort vs. ordinary effort (comparison). They'll likely express despair next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student requested distinction ('In what way does super-effort differ from an ordinary effort?')—seeking criterion for difference via requests distinction and seeks comparison. They understand: super-effort vs. ordinary effort (comparison). The comparison_contrast mechanism refines: super-effort = conscious + purposive + non-required (complete definition), ordinary effort = pretence (devalued). Pattern: definition question → definition → distinction question → refinement → [next: despair question]. They're grasping: super-effort = conscious/purposive/non-required, ordinary = pretence (refinement).
visible_response:
It differs in degree, but not only in degree.
Super-effort is effort made consciously,
as much as we can, for a definite purpose which is not
required by any external circumstances.
We never really make serious efforts; it is all
pretence, for we do not know what it means to make efforts.
In exceptional conditions,
when we are obliged to make efforts, we make them, but not super-efforts.
Besides,
ordinary efforts one makes in life are necessary, useful.
A super-effort may look useless for its purpose.
